text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Renata Vasquez-Okonkwo
MRN: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
DATE OF BIRTH: [REDACTED]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED], MD — Medical Oncology
INTERPRETING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD — GI/Hepatic Pathology
CLINICAL SERVICE: Medical Oncology
REFERRING SERVICE: Interventional Radiology (Image-Guided Procedures)
PATHOLOGY CASE NUMBER: [REDACTED]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CLINICAL HISTORY:
The patient is a 57-year-old female with a known history of Stage IV (M1c) BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic cutaneous melanoma, superficial spreading type, originally arising from the left posterior shoulder. She was initially treated with dabrafenib plus trametinib (first-line), achieving a partial response prior to progression at approximately 8 months. She subsequently received second-line ipilimumab (1 mg/kg) plus nivolumab (3 mg/kg) x 4 induction cycles, which was complicated by grade 3 immune-mediated colitis requiring permanent discontinuation of ipilimumab; she was then continued on nivolumab maintenance (480 mg IV every 4 weeks). Restaging CT and MRI performed at approximately 12 months of nivolumab maintenance (15 months from initiation of combination immunotherapy) demonstrated near-complete response by RECIST 1.1 criteria, with a single residual sub-centimeter hepatic lesion in the right lobe (0.4 cm on CT), all other previously documented sites of metastatic disease having resolved. Liver biopsy is submitted for histopathologic assessment of treatment response and viability of residual lesion.

PROCEDURE DATE: [REDACTED]

TYPE OF SPECIMEN:
Image-guided (CT-fluoroscopy) percutaneous core needle biopsy, right hepatic lobe, segment VI/VII, targeting a 0.4 cm residual hypoattenuating lesion identified on recent contrast-enhanced CT.

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
Specimen A: "Liver core biopsy, right lobe, segment VI" — received as two tan-white to buff-colored cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments, each placed in 10% neutral buffered formalin.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and "liver core biopsy, right lobe, segment VI." The container holds two tan-white to buff-colored cylindrical tissue cores. Core 1 measures 1.3 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter. Core 2 measures 1.1 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter. The cores are firm in consistency. The cut surfaces are tan-white and homogeneous, without grossly discernible hemorrhage, necrosis, or nodular lesion. The tissue cores are entirely submitted for histologic examination.

Entire specimen submitted in one block:
  A1: Both tissue cores, entirely submitted.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Histologic sections show fragments of hepatic parenchyma with preserved lobular architecture. Portal tracts are present and unremarkable in number and composition. The hepatocytes demonstrate mild lobular disarray without significant cytologic atypia, ballooning degeneration, or cholestasis. There is mild sinusoidal dilation.

Within the sampled parenchyma, there is a discrete area of dense fibrosis with associated hyalinization, occupying approximately 30-40% of the biopsy core. This fibrotic region contains scattered clusters of pigment-laden macrophages (consistent with melanin-laden histiocytes) and deposits of hemosiderin within Kupffer cells and macrophages, consistent with prior hemorrhagic necrosis. Focal dystrophic calcification is present at the periphery of this fibrotic zone. The fibrous tissue appears mature and collagenized, without active inflammatory infiltration.

Within and immediately adjacent to the fibrotic area, there is a sparse lymphohistiocytic infiltrate in the sinusoids and portal tracts. No granulomata are identified. No significant neutrophilic infiltrate or frank abscess formation is present.

Critically, no viable epithelioid or spindled tumor cells are identified. No nests, sheets, or individual cells with the morphologic features of malignant melanoma — including enlarged nuclei, prominent nucleoli, dusty melanin pigmentation, or atypical mitotic figures — are identified within the sections examined.

Background liver parenchyma: The non-fibrotic hepatic parenchyma shows mild macrovesicular steatosis (estimated 5-10%), mild lobular inflammation (grade 1 by METAVIR), and portal fibrosis score F1 (mild) without established bridging fibrosis or cirrhosis. These findings are likely attributable to prior therapeutic exposure and/or metabolic background. No features of autoimmune hepatitis or drug-induced liver injury with active hepatocyte necrosis are identified, though the clinical setting of prior immune checkpoint inhibitor use is noted.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC):
A panel of melanocytic immunostains was performed on block A1.

  HMB-45 (gp100):       NEGATIVE
                        No immunoreactivity in any spindle or epithelioid cells
                        within the fibrotic zone or surrounding hepatic parenchyma.

  Melan-A (MART-1):     NEGATIVE
                        No immunoreactivity in any cells within the specimen.
                        Internal control (hepatocytes): not applicable.

  S100 protein:         FOCALLY POSITIVE (non-specific/reactive pattern)
                        Weak S100 positivity identified only in scattered
                        dendritic-type cells, Schwann cell processes in portal
                        nerve twigs, and stromal fibroblasts within the fibrotic
                        zone. No S100 positivity in cells with morphologic
                        features of melanoma. The pattern is consistent with
                        reactive/stromal S100 staining and not indicative of
                        residual viable melanoma.

  SOX10:                NEGATIVE
                        No nuclear immunoreactivity in cells within the fibrotic
                        area or surrounding parenchyma. No SOX10-positive
                        tumor cells identified.

  Ki-67 (MIB-1):        Less than 1% nuclear positivity in fibrotic zone.
                        No significant proliferative activity is noted within
                        the area of interest.

ADDITIONAL STAINS:
  Prussian Blue (iron): POSITIVE — Iron deposition in macrophages and Kupffer
                        cells, consistent with hemosiderin from prior
                        hemorrhagic necrosis/tumor regression.

  Masson's Trichrome:   Dense mature collagen deposition within the fibrotic
                        zone confirmed; no active fibroblastic proliferation
                        ("blue collagen" consistent with mature/established scar).

  PAS with diastase:    No abnormal glycogen or PAS-positive organisms identified.
                        No evidence of fungal organisms.

MOLECULAR/ADDITIONAL STUDIES:
  BRAF V600E IHC        PENDING (VE1 clone; ordered as confirmatory ancillary
  (VE1):                test; result to be reported in addendum when available)

  NOTE: Additional molecular testing (e.g., repeat NGS on current specimen) is
  deferred, as no viable tumor cells are identified for analysis. Insufficient
  viable tumor material for nucleic acid extraction.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DIAGNOSIS:

A. LIVER, RIGHT LOBE (SEGMENT VI/VII), CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

   - NO VIABLE MELANOMA IDENTIFIED.

   - FIBROTIC AND NECROTIC TISSUE WITH PIGMENT-LADEN MACROPHAGES AND
     HEMOSIDERIN DEPOSITION, CONSISTENT WITH TREATED/REGRESSED MELANOMA
     (PATHOLOGIC COMPLETE RESPONSE AT THIS SITE).

   - MELANOCYTIC IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (HMB-45, MELAN-A, SOX10) NEGATIVE;
     S100 FOCALLY POSITIVE IN REACTIVE STROMAL/DENDRITIC CELLS ONLY
     (NON-SPECIFIC PATTERN; SEE MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION).

   - MILD BACKGROUND STEATOHEPATITIS (GRADE 1, STAGE F1) AND
     MILD MACROVESICULAR STEATOSIS IN UNINVOLVED PARENCHYMA.

   - BRAF V600E IHC (VE1 CLONE): PENDING — SEE ADDENDUM.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

COMMENT:

The histomorphologic and immunophenotypic findings in this core biopsy are consistent with complete pathologic response (pCR) at this hepatic site. The discrete zone of dense, mature collagenous fibrosis with associated hemosiderin deposition and pigment-laden macrophages without viable melanoma cells is a well-recognized pattern of treatment-induced tumor regression in melanoma following immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy. The complete absence of immunoreactivity for HMB-45, Melan-A, and SOX10 — all sensitive and specific melanocytic markers — and the absence of S100-positive cells with melanoma morphology support the interpretation of no residual viable melanoma.

The focal S100 positivity observed is confined to reactive stromal elements (dendritic cells, portal nerve fibers), a pattern commonly encountered in regressed or necrotic tumor beds and not indicative of viable melanoma.

The degree of background hepatic fibrosis (F1) and mild steatosis are noted as incidental findings in the uninvolved parenchyma, likely related to prior treatment effects (BRAFi/MEKi exposure, systemic inflammation from immune-mediated colitis) and/or metabolic factors.

This case was reviewed and signed out by the staff pathologist. Correlation with clinical and radiographic findings is recommended, as always.

Addendum pending: BRAF V600E IHC (VE1) result will be reported separately upon completion.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SIGNED:
[REDACTED], MD
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
GI/Hepatic Pathology Division

Electronic signature applied.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS ISSUED.
ADDENDUM PENDING (BRAF V600E IHC).
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
